title: salvia verbenaca var. praecox
intro: Salvia verbenaca var. praecox, also known as early blue sage, is a perennial plant species in the sage family (Lamiaceae) and is native to Europe and North Africa. Its medicinal properties are attributed to its essential oils, which have been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory ailments. The plant's health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of certain microorganisms in the body. Salvia verbenaca var. praecox can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed for therapeutic use.
